title: Richly flavored Mediterranean lamb Tagine
description: A cultured and dense weeknight American dish featuring lamb stock and nuka-pickle, served in a scallop shell with Mediterranean-friendly flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb stock, nuka-pickle, dried apricots, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, spooned into scallop shells and garnished with fresh herbs, if desired.
